incidents.vector: Unknown intrusion into the national healthcare database; CNAM has not disclosed the entry method.
incidents.impact: Threat actors stole sensitive personal and financial data on virtually every Moldovan citizen and foreign resident, plus financial details of private medical providers; roughly 30% of the database was impacted, with Moldova's cyber agency saying Russian involvement cannot be excluded amid escalating Russian pressure on the country.

incidents.vector: Compromise of an on-premises Microsoft Exchange email server, part of a broader campaign that hit more than 5,000 Exchange servers this year.
incidents.impact: Attackers exfiltrated Cuban diplomats' email inboxes, reportedly to gather intelligence on the U.S. naval blockade of Cuba, with collateral exposure across thousands of other Exchange-using organizations.

incidents.vector: Mirai-variant botnet built from TP-Link Archer AX21 routers vulnerable to CVE-2023-1389, operated through compromised infrastructure and SSH keys belonging to Brazilian DDoS-mitigation firm Huge Networks.
incidents.impact: Sustained large-scale DDoS attacks against small Brazilian ISPs over multiple years, leveraging DNS reflection and amplification with Huge Networks' own infrastructure; the firm's CEO blames a January 2026 intrusion and an unnamed competitor.

incidents.vector: Ransomware intrusion details not disclosed; data subsequently leaked on the SafePay extortion site.
incidents.impact: Stolen corporate data was published on the SafePay leak site after Favelle Favco apparently declined to pay, exposing internal documents from the heavy-crane manufacturer.

incidents.vector: Data was originally stolen earlier in 2026 as part of ShinyHunters' campaign against multiple consumer brands; specific intrusion vector at Carnival has not been disclosed.
incidents.impact: Personal data on 7.5 million Carnival cruise customers was leaked publicly by ShinyHunters; the same wave hit Zara, 7-Eleven, Udemy and Medtronic.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into OneIndia's digital streaming platforms used to broadcast a political message; technical entry point not disclosed.
incidents.impact: An Afghan hacktivist group hijacked OneIndia's streaming output to attack the Indian government's ties with the Taliban regime; OneIndia has more than 30 million subscribers.

incidents.vector: Cyber incident with characteristics consistent with ransomware; details not disclosed.
incidents.impact: One of West Virginia's largest counties disclosed a cybersecurity incident affecting its IT operations, with response and recovery still underway.

incidents.vector: Voice-phishing (vishing) attack that compromised an employee's Okta single-sign-on account, giving the attackers access to internal systems.
incidents.impact: Cybercriminals stole names, phone numbers, addresses, dates of birth, and the last four digits of Social Security and tax IDs for a 'limited' subset of customers; the group claims 10 million records and has threatened to leak data unless paid. ADT says alarm-monitoring services were unaffected.

incidents.vector: Signal account takeover via a phishing-style attack consistent with the Russian campaign Germany has flagged against Signal and WhatsApp users.
incidents.impact: Russian operators reportedly accessed a sensitive Signal group involving Chancellor Friedrich Merz and other senior officials via Klöckner's compromised account; Merz's own account was not breached.

incidents.vector: Diversion of an outgoing foreign-debt repayment; specific compromise method not disclosed publicly.
incidents.impact: Attackers redirected $2.5 million in debt repayments destined for Australia; Sri Lanka has formally asked Australian law enforcement to assist with the investigation.

incidents.vector: Source of compromise not disclosed; data ultimately surfaced for sale via the Alibaba marketplace.
incidents.impact: Personal medical data on roughly 500,000 volunteers in the UK Biobank was offered for sale on Alibaba, raising concerns about how a database of voluntarily-donated health data leaked into criminal channels.

incidents.vector: Compromise of an employee account, likely via phishing or credential theft.
incidents.impact: Canadian life insurer disclosed that customer data was stolen after an employee account was hijacked; the company says the incident is contained while it investigates the scope of stolen data.

incidents.vector: Compromise details not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Dutch cosmetics company disclosed a breach exposing customer personal data across European markets; no passwords or payment data were compromised.

incidents.vector: Theft of npm tokens and GitHub Personal Access Tokens used to publish a malicious @bitwarden/cli package and poison Checkmarx's Docker images, GitHub Actions and VS Code extensions, all communicating with audit.checkmarx[.]cx.
incidents.impact: TeamPCP's coordinated supply-chain campaign — a sequel to the September 2025 Shai-Hulud worm — propagated credential-stealing payloads via trusted developer tooling, partnered with the Vect ransomware crew to monetize victims, and prompted urgent token-rotation advisories for downstream consumers.

incidents.victim: U.S. federal civilian agencies and global government / defense networks
incidents.vector: Exploitation of n-day Cisco ASA/Firepower vulnerabilities CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362 to deploy the FIRESTARTER backdoor, which manipulates the Cisco Service Platform mount list to survive firmware patches and standard reboots.
incidents.impact: CISA found FIRESTARTER on a federal civilian agency's Cisco firewall and updated Emergency Directive 25-03; Unit 42 saw scanning of 12 federal and 11 state/local IPs plus government IPs across more than a dozen allied countries; CISA's follow-up directive notes agencies that thought they were patched are still running vulnerable software.

incidents.vector: Large-scale 'covert networks' built from compromised SOHO routers, IoT and smart devices (e.g., Raptor Train, KV Botnet) that proxy reconnaissance, malware delivery, command-and-control and data exfiltration.
incidents.impact: A 12-country joint advisory (NCSC-UK, CISA, NSA, FBI, ACSC and partners) warns that Chinese info-security firms create and maintain these botnets — used by groups associated with Volt Typhoon and Flax Typhoon — to pre-position on critical infrastructure and run espionage with deniable infrastructure that defeats static IP block-lists.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack on the city's IT network with characteristics consistent with ransomware; specific entry vector not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Florida's capital shut down its IT network and several public-facing services; surrounding Leon County disconnected its own network to prevent contamination, a typical precaution for ransomware events.

incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — data extortion (ShinyHunters claim, attribution disputed)
incidents.vector: Lumma Stealer infostealer hidden in a Roblox cheat infected a Context.ai employee in February, leading to theft of OAuth tokens — including a Vercel employee's Google Workspace token granted 'Allow All' permissions to Context AI Office Suite — which the attackers then used to pivot into Vercel.
incidents.impact: Attackers traversed Vercel internal systems, enumerated environment variables and triggered credential-rotation alerts to a 'small number' of Vercel customers; ShinyHunters claims to be selling Vercel access keys, source code and databases for $2 million while Vercel works with Mandiant on response.

incidents.victim: Israeli national water and desalination operators
incidents.vector: ZionSiphon malware targeting OT software used to manage water-treatment and desalination equipment, hardcoded to run only on Israeli IP ranges and against Israeli operator names.
incidents.impact: Designed to push pump pressure and chlorine levels to unsafe maximums against Tel Aviv and Haifa water systems, the malware embeds Iran-aligned political strings but currently fails to trigger because of an XOR encoding bug in its IP-range check; Darktrace cannot confirm in-the-wild deployment.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of a smart-contract vulnerability that allowed unauthorized withdrawals of rsETH (restaked Ether) tokens.
incidents.impact: Theft of $292 million in crypto assets — the largest crypto heist of 2026 to date — wiping a substantial share of the protocol's holdings.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of vulnerabilities in Rhea Finance's margin-trading feature, prepared days in advance via fake token pools used to route the attack.
incidents.impact: Theft of $18.4 million in digital assets from the DeFi protocol; staged token pools and routing tricks are increasingly common in DeFi-protocol exploits.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into internal corporate systems; specific attack vector not publicly disclosed.
incidents.impact: Itron disclosed and remediated a security breach detected on April 13; the company says cyber insurance will cover most of the costs and has not described data exposure.
